title: Evacuation plan for limited mobility
summary: Pre-arrange routes, helpers, equipment, and fallback shelter so stairs, narrow exits, or a failed lift do not trap a person.
content:
Pre-arrange routes, helpers, equipment, and fallback shelter so stairs, narrow exits, or a failed lift do not trap a person.

Action sequence: Measure doors, stairs, landings, and the realistic weight and turning space of the mobility device. Choose a primary route, an alternative route, and a protected waiting point known to responders. Write how to move, fold, power, or avoid damaging the device and which actions require trained responders. Practise communication and movement without lifting or carrying methods that helpers have not been trained to use. Safety boundary: Do not use a lift during fire or when authorities say it is unsafe. Do not separate a person from an essential aid unless immediate life safety requires it.
steps: Measure doors, stairs, landings, and the realistic weight and turning space of the mobility device.
Choose a primary route, an alternative route, and a protected waiting point known to responders.
Write how to move, fold, power, or avoid damaging the device and which actions require trained responders.
Practise communication and movement without lifting or carrying methods that helpers have not been trained to use.
warnings: Do not use a lift during fire or when authorities say it is unsafe.
Do not separate a person from an essential aid unless immediate life safety requires it.
common_mistakes: Starting “Evacuation plan for limited mobility” without the listed materials or first safety check.
Continuing after this warning appears: Do not use a lift during fire or when authorities say it is unsafe.

Leaving the final decision, time, or responsible person undocumented. Topic-specific verification for “Evacuation plan for limited mobility”: before closing the card, confirm completion of “Practise communication and movement without lifting or carrying methods that helpers have not been trained to use.”.
alternatives: If “mobility-aid details card” is unavailable, use a clean, clearly labelled substitute only when it serves the same safety function.

When the full card cannot be completed, perform the first safe action, record the limitation, and transfer the unresolved part to 112, the competent authority, or a qualified professional. The safest reduced starting action for “Evacuation plan for limited mobility” is: “Measure doors, stairs, landings, and the realistic weight and turning space of the mobility device.”.
failure_signs: The plan for “Evacuation plan for limited mobility” no longer matches the real location, people, hazard, or official instruction.

A required person, route, supply, communication method, or safety boundary cannot be verified. For “Evacuation plan for limited mobility”, reassess immediately against this warning: “Do not separate a person from an essential aid unless immediate life safety requires it.”.
when_not_to_use: Do not use “Evacuation plan for limited mobility” as a substitute for 112, evacuation orders, poison-centre advice, medical care, or qualified utility and structural assessment.

Do not continue when the scene exposes people to fire, smoke, floodwater, live electricity, gas, violence, unstable structures, or another uncontrolled hazard. Topic-specific stop check for “Evacuation plan for limited mobility”: also follow this warning: “Do not use a lift during fire or when authorities say it is unsafe.”.
short_term: Use “Evacuation plan for limited mobility” for the current operational period, then reassess after every alert, route change, symptom, utility change, loss of contact, or new hazard.
long_term: Practise “Evacuation plan for limited mobility” at least seasonally, replace expired supplies, update contacts and access needs, and keep one offline paper version with a second trained person.
if_method_fails: If “Evacuation plan for limited mobility” fails, stop depending on the failed route or resource, protect people from the immediate consequence, communicate the exact gap, and move to the safest verified fallback or qualified service.

title: Emergency communication card for deaf or hard-of-hearing people
summary: Prepare visual and text-based communication options for alarms, responders, shelters, and family contact.
content:
Prepare visual and text-based communication options for alarms, responders, shelters, and family contact.

Action sequence: Write preferred language, communication method, emergency contacts, medicines, allergies, and whether an interpreter is required. Enable vibration, flash, and emergency-alert settings and test them with another person. Prepare short phrases for HELP, FIRE, MEDICAL, TRAPPED, SAFE, LOCATION, and NEED INTERPRETER in Greek and English. Show the card early to responders and confirm instructions in writing or by read-back. Safety boundary: Do not assume a siren, shouted instruction, or phone call has been received. Do not remove hearing equipment without the person’s direction unless immediate danger requires it.
materials: flashlight. Context: “Emergency communication card for deaf or hard-of-hearing people”.

Enable vibration, flash, and emergency-alert settings and test them with another person.
Prepare short phrases for HELP, FIRE, MEDICAL, TRAPPED, SAFE, LOCATION, and NEED INTERPRETER in Greek and English.
Show the card early to responders and confirm instructions in writing or by read-back.
warnings: Do not assume a siren, shouted instruction, or phone call has been received.
Do not remove hearing equipment without the person’s direction unless immediate danger requires it.

title: Emergency layout for blind or low-vision household members
summary: Keep routes, labels, objects, and verbal guidance predictable so sudden clutter or relocation does not create a second hazard.
content:
Keep routes, labels, objects, and verbal guidance predictable so sudden clutter or relocation does not create a second hazard.

Action sequence: Keep the normal route to exits free and avoid moving furniture without informing the person. Use tactile or high-contrast labels for emergency bag, medicines, water, keys, and utility controls. When guiding, offer an elbow, describe steps and obstacles before reaching them, and let the person control pace. Create an alternative route and practise it with the usual mobility aid and service animal if applicable. Safety boundary: Do not pull, push, or grab the person or their cane without warning. Do not separate a service animal from its handler except for an immediate uncontrollable hazard.

title: Low-stress emergency plan for cognitive or sensory needs
summary: Reduce overload with a short visual sequence, familiar objects, one communicator, and predictable choices.
content:
Reduce overload with a short visual sequence, familiar objects, one communicator, and predictable choices.

Action sequence: Create a five-step sequence: shoes, bag, exit, meeting point, contact. Identify triggers, calming methods, wandering risk, communication style, and essential routines. Use one calm speaker, short concrete phrases, extra processing time, and two safe choices rather than many questions. Place identification and support details inside clothing or the emergency bag with privacy considered. Safety boundary: Do not restrain, threaten, or punish distress behaviour unless trained responders must prevent immediate harm. Do not remove essential communication or comfort items merely to make the kit lighter.

Identify triggers, calming methods, wandering risk, communication style, and essential routines.
Use one calm speaker, short concrete phrases, extra processing time, and two safe choices rather than many questions.
Place identification and support details inside clothing or the emergency bag with privacy considered.
warnings: Do not restrain, threaten, or punish distress behaviour unless trained responders must prevent immediate harm.
Do not remove essential communication or comfort items merely to make the kit lighter.

title: Backup plan for electricity-dependent medical devices
summary: Document power demand, battery duration, safe charging, clinical contacts, and relocation triggers before an outage.
content:
Document power demand, battery duration, safe charging, clinical contacts, and relocation triggers before an outage.

Action sequence: Record device voltage, power use, battery runtime, alarms, consumables, and whether interruption is immediately dangerous. Test each approved backup source under supervision of the supplier or clinical team and label connection steps. Set a battery threshold and outage duration that triggers contact with 112, the clinician, supplier, or a powered safe location. Keep the device, tubing, batteries, documents, and transport aid together and ready to move. Safety boundary: Do not connect a medical device to an improvised or incompatible power source. Do not wait for the last battery alarm before arranging qualified help or relocation.
